messages.content: Hello! How can I help you today?
Hello! How can I help you today?Hey! 👋 Nice to hear from you. How's your day going? Anything on your mind, or just taking a break?

function.name: browser_fetch
function.description: Fetch URL with headless browser. Modes: text (default), html, screenshot, markdown. Use waitFor for dynamic content.

viewport.description: Override default viewport size. Defaults to 1920x1080.

function.name: browser_evaluate
function.description: Execute JavaScript in page context

function.name: browser_pdf
function.description: Generate PDF of page

function.name: browser_session_create
function.description: Create a persistent browser session. Returns a sessionId in the response text. Use this sessionId in all subsequent browser_session_* calls (goto, content, click, fill, evaluate, scroll, metadata, close). Example: call this first, then use browser_session_goto with the returned sessionId.

function.name: browser_session_list
function.description: List all active browser sessions

function.name: browser_session_close
function.description: Close a specific browser session

function.name: browser_session_goto
function.description: Navigate to a URL. Requires sessionId from browser_session_create. Returns confirmation with the URL navigated to. Has built-in retry with exponential backoff for flaky loads.

waitFor.description: CSS selector to wait for after navigation

function.name: browser_session_content
function.description: Get page content. mode 'text' extracts readable text, 'screenshot' returns base64 image, 'html' returns raw HTML, 'markdown' returns markdown-formatted content. Requires sessionId from browser_session_create.

function.name: browser_session_click
function.description: Click an element by CSS selector. Returns page content after the click (use mode to get screenshot or text). Requires sessionId from browser_session_create. Has built-in retry for flaky elements.

function.name: browser_session_fill
function.description: Fill form fields and optionally submit. Each field has a CSS selector and value. If submit selector is provided, clicks it after filling. Returns page content. Requires sessionId from browser_session_create. Has built-in retry for flaky forms.

function.name: browser_session_evaluate
function.description: Execute JavaScript in the page context. Script must return a value (string, number, or JSON-serializable object). Requires sessionId from browser_session_create.

function.name: browser_session_scroll
function.description: Scroll the page. direction 'down' or 'up', amount in pixels (default 500). Requires sessionId from browser_session_create.

function.name: browser_session_type
function.description: Type text into a focused element or simulate keystrokes. Sends key events character-by-character, which triggers input validators and dynamic form handlers better than direct value setting. Use for CAPTCHAs, autocomplete, or sites that validate on key events.

selector.description: CSS selector to focus before typing (optional - if omitted, types into currently focused element)

delay.description: Delay between keystrokes in ms (default 0, use 50-100 for slow sites)

keystrokes.description: Special key presses: Enter, Tab, Escape, ArrowUp, ArrowDown, ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Backspace, Delete, Home, End, PageUp, PageDown

function.name: browser_session_inspect
function.description: Get detailed info about an element: tag, attributes, text content, position, visibility, and an optional sub-screenshot cropped to the element. Useful for debugging selectors and understanding element state.

function.name: browser_session_console
function.description: Get console messages and errors captured from the page JavaScript since last call or session start. Buffers messages per session. Returns all accumulated messages and clears the buffer.

function.name: browser_session_wait
function.description: Enhanced waiting with multiple options: wait for CSS selector (with OR logic for multiple), text content in page, URL pattern matching, or custom condition via JS. More flexible than waitFor in goto.

selectors.description: CSS selectors - waits for ANY to appear (OR logic)

text.description: Text to wait for in page content

urlPattern.description: Regex pattern to match against current URL

condition.description: JS expression that must evaluate to truthy

function.name: browser_session_metadata
function.description: Get current page metadata: URL, title, and viewport dimensions. Useful to verify navigation succeeded. Requires sessionId from browser_session_create.

function.name: list_codebases
function.description: List all indexed codebases with status and file counts

function.name: list_spaces
function.description: List available spaces (configured project roots) for indexing codebases. Use this to find space names before calling index_codebase.

function.name: search_codebase
function.description: [RECOMMENDED] Primary focused search tool for a single codebase. Very fast. Combines semantic (meaning) + keyword (exact) matching. ALWAYS use this first before falling back to search_all_codebases. Use strategy:keyword for fast exact name lookups, strategy:semantic for conceptual queries. Use analyze:true to get an AI-summarized response (saves tokens). Codebase name supports partial matching.

strategy.description: hybrid=best overall, keyword=fast exact match, semantic=conceptual similarity

analyze.description: Use local LLM to analyze results and return structured summary with keyFindings, relevantFiles, implementationPatterns. Saves 50-90% tokens vs raw results. Recommended for exploration.

includeRaw.description: Include raw search results in addition to analysis. Use when you want both the AI summary AND original snippets for drilling down.

function.name: search_semantic
function.description: Semantic (AI embedding) search - finds conceptually similar code. Best for: "how is X implemented?", "find code that does Y", pattern discovery. Slower than keyword but understands meaning, not just text. Use analyze:true for structured summary. Codebase name supports partial matching.

query.description: Natural language query describing what you are looking for

analyze.description: Use local LLM to analyze results and return structured summary with keyFindings, relevantFiles, implementationPatterns. Saves 50-90% tokens vs raw results. Recommended for exploration.

includeRaw.description: Include raw search results in addition to analysis. Use when you want both the AI summary AND original snippets for drilling down.

function.name: search_keyword
function.description: [FASTEST] Indexed keyword search. Best for: exact function names, class names, variable names, imports. Searches file paths AND content. Much faster than grep_codebase (<100ms vs 1-3s). Use this INSTEAD of grep_codebase when searching for specific identifiers. Codebase name supports partial matching.

query.description: Keywords to search (function names, class names, etc.)

searchContent.description: Search file content (not just paths). Slightly slower but more thorough.

analyze.description: Use local LLM to analyze results and return structured summary (reduces token cost for calling LLM)

includeRaw.description: Include raw search results in addition to analysis (increases token usage)

function.name: grep_codebase
function.description: Live regex search using ripgrep. ALWAYS CURRENT (searches filesystem directly). Slowest single-codebase search. Use ONLY when: (1) You need complex regex patterns, (2) You need exact line numbers for editing, (3) You strongly suspect the index is stale. OTHERWISE prefer search_keyword (faster for exact names) or search_semantic (for concepts). Codebase name supports partial matching.

regex.description: Use regex pattern matching. Set to false for literal string search (faster)

maxMatchesPerFile.description: Max matches per file (-1 for unlimited, 1 for "find files only")

noCache.description: Skip cache and force fresh search

analyze.description: Use local LLM to analyze results and return structured summary with keyFindings, relevantFiles, implementationPatterns. Saves 50-90% tokens vs raw results.

includeRaw.description: Include raw search results in addition to analysis. Use when you want both the AI summary AND original snippets for drilling down.

function.name: search_all_codebases
function.description: [AVOID IF POSSIBLE] Slowest search method. Search across ALL indexed codebases at once. ONLY use this as a last resort when focused searches on individual codebases have failed, or for extremely broad queries like "how is X implemented across different projects?" Use analyze:true to get AI-summarized insights instead of raw snippets.

query.description: Search query (natural language, keywords, or grep pattern)

strategy.description: hybrid=best overall (default), keyword=fastest, semantic=conceptual, grep=slow regex (avoid unless needed)

limit.description: Total result limit across all codebases

concurrency.description: Number of codebases to search in parallel

analyze.description: Use local LLM to analyze results and return structured summary with keyFindings, relevantFiles, implementationPatterns. Saves 50-90% tokens vs raw results. STRONGLY RECOMMENDED for cross-codebase exploration.

includeRaw.description: Include raw search results in addition to analysis. Use when you want both the AI summary AND original snippets for drilling down.

function.name: get_file_info
function.description: Get file structure (functions, classes, imports) without content. Codebase name supports partial matching.

function.name: get_file
function.description: Get file content with staleness check. Codebase name supports partial matching.

function.name: check_codebase_status
function.description: Check staleness status of a codebase (stale files, missing files). Codebase name supports partial matching.

function.name: check_file_stale
function.description: Check if a specific file is stale (changed since indexing). Codebase name supports partial matching.

path.description: File path relative to codebase root

function.name: get_codebase_description
function.description: Get LLM-generated project description with staleness check. Codebase name supports partial matching.

function.name: get_prioritized_files
function.description: Get files ordered by importance (high/medium/low priority). Codebase name supports partial matching.

function.name: get_documentation
function.description: Get the main orchestrator documentation (tools guide). Equivalent to read_document({name: 'orchestrator'}).

function.name: list_documents
function.description: List all available documentation files in the mcp_documentation folder.

function.name: read_document
function.description: Read a specific documentation file by name (without .md extension).

function.name: inspect_code
function.description: Analyze existing code files iteratively. Finds bugs, explains architecture, or suggests improvements. File paths must be absolute.

files.description: Absolute paths of files to inspect (e.g. 'D:\Project\src\main.js').

prompt.description: Specific question or analysis request for these files.

function.name: query_model
function.description: Query the local LLM. To include files, use absolute paths.

systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt. Overrides the default assistant prompt.

function.name: remember
function.description: Store a memory. Categories: proven, anti_patterns, hypotheses, context, observed. Use domain for project-specific memories.

function.name: recall
function.description: Search memories. Results show confidence: [v]=proven, ~=promising, ?=hypothesis.

function.name: forget
function.description: Delete a memory by ID

function.name: list_memories
function.description: List all memories. Filter by category/domain.

function.name: update_memory
function.description: Update a memory by ID

function.name: reflect_on_session
function.description: USER-INITIATED. Analyze session for quality improvements. Returns proposed changes.

function.name: apply_reflection_changes
function.description: Apply changes from reflection

function.name: research_topic
function.description: Research a topic via web search. Multi-phase: search, scrape, synthesize with citations.

query.description: Search query (quote terms, use site: filters)

function.name: vision_create_session
function.description: Create a new image session for analysis. Returns a session_id to use with vision_analyze. The session stores the original image and accumulates analyses over time.

image_url.description: Image URL (remote or data URL). Either this or image_data is required.

image_data.description: Base64-encoded image data. Alternative to image_url.

image_mime_type.description: MIME type (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'image/png'). Required if using image_data.

function.name: vision_analyze
function.description: Analyze an image. Previous analyses are included as context, enabling ever-sharpening detail. Use 'focus' to zoom into specific regions for higher resolution analysis of that area.

query.description: What to analyze. Examples: 'Describe everything', 'What is the person wearing?', 'Read the text on the sign'

focus.description: Optional: Region to focus on for higher resolution analysis

text.description: Free text focus: 'top-left corner', 'person on the left', 'the sign in the background'

grid.description: Grid-based focus: divide image into cols x rows, analyze specific cells

cells.description: Cell indices to analyze (0=top-left, read left-to-right, top-to-bottom)

region.description: Normalized pixel region for precise cropping

oneOf.description: Crop to center N% (e.g., 50 = keep center 50%)

include_context.description: Whether to include previous analyses as context

function.name: vision_close_session
function.description: Close an image session and free memory. Sessions auto-expire after 30 minutes of inactivity, but this frees resources immediately.

function.name: vision_list_sessions
function.description: List all active image sessions

function.name: vision_get_session
function.description: Get details about a session including accumulated analyses
